title: Flow Cytometric Quantification of HIV-1-Infected Cells Using Flow-FISH
author: Shirley Man
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML
materials: Primeflow RNA Assay kit (Invitrogen, catalog number: 88-18005), containing: a. RNA tubes (1.5-mL microcentrifuge tubes) (Invitrogen, catalog number:

b. Fixation buffer 1A (Invitrogen, catalog number: 00-18100); store at 2–8 °C c. Fixation buffer 1B (Invitrogen, catalog number: 00-18200); store at 2–8 °C d. Fixation buffer 2 (8×) (Invitrogen, catalog number: 00-18400); store at 2–8 °C e. Permeabilization buffer (10×) (Invitrogen, catalog number: 00-18300); store at 2–8 °C f. RNase inhibitor (100×) (Invitrogen, catalog number: 00-16002); store at 2–8 °C g. Wash buffer (Invitrogen, catalog number: 00-19180); store at 2–8 °C h. Target probe diluent (Invitrogen, catalog number: 00-19185); store at 2–8 °C i. PreAmp mix (Invitrogen, catalog number: 00-16000); store at 2–8 °C j. Amp mix (Invitrogen, catalog number: 00-16001); store at 2–8 °C k. Label probe diluent (Invitrogen, catalog number: 00-19183); store at 2–8 °C l. Label probes (100×) (Invitrogen, catalog number: 00-16003); store at -20°C m. Intracellular (IC) fixation buffer (Invitrogen, catalog number: 00-8222); store at 2–8 °C n. Storage buffer (Invitrogen, catalog number: 00-19178); store at 2–8 °C o. Compensation kit (Invitrogen, catalog number: 88-
UltraComp eBeads TM microspheres (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Invitrogen, catalog number: 01-2222); store at 2–8 °C
PrimeFlow TM Compensation Control Alexa Fluor TM 647 (Invitrogen, catalog number: PF51-17002); store at 2–8 °C
PrimeFlow TM Compensation Control Alexa Fluor TM 488 (Invitrogen, catalog number: PF51-17003); store at 2–8 °C

Primeflow microRNA pretreatment buffer (Invitrogen) a. Pretreatment concentrate (4×) (Invitrogen, catalog number: 00-16009); store at 2–8 °C b. Pretreatment diluent (Invitrogen, catalog number: 00-16008); store at 2–8 °C
CD4-PE antibody (RPA-T4 clone) (BioLegend, catalog number: 300507); store at 2–8 °C
Phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), 1×, pH ~7.4 (Thermo Fisher Scientific, catalog number: 10010023); store at room temperature

steps.title: ACH-2, a latently HIV-1-infected T-cell line...
steps.instruction: ACH-2, a latently HIV-1-infected T-cell line derived from CEM cells (NIH AIDS Reagent Program; RRID: CVCL_

steps.title: PBMCs from PWH and an HIV-negative...
steps.instruction: PBMCs from PWH and an HIV-negative blood donor; isolated fresh or frozen Reagents

steps.title: Target probe sets, 20× (custom-made, Affymetrix,...
steps.instruction: Target probe sets, 20× (custom-made, Affymetrix, Santa Clara, CA, USA; see Table S1 for used target sequences from HIV-1 clade B); store at -20 °C a. HIV-1 TAR in Alexa Fluor 647 b. HIV-1 5′LTR in Alexa Fluor 647 c. HIV-1 Gag in Alexa Fluor 488

steps.title: FACS buffer (store at 4 °C)...
steps.instruction: FACS buffer (store at 4 °C) Reagent Final concentration PBS BSA 0.3% EDTA 2 mM Sodium azide 0.01%

steps.title: Section E can be prepared 5 days prior to acquisition
steps.instruction: Section E can be prepared 5 days prior to acquisition. Moreover, we strongly recommend including an HIV-negative control and a probe-signal positive control for each individual experiment. In this protocol, we verified the use of PBMCs from blood donors and reactivated ACH-2 cells as negative and positive assay controls, respectively. A. Cell sample preparation

steps.title: Optional positive control
steps.instruction: Optional positive control: seed 5 × 10 6 ACH-2 cells at a density of 1 × 10 6 cells per mL on Day

steps.title: Prewarm PrimeFlow RNA wash buffer to room temperature
steps.instruction: Prewarm PrimeFlow RNA wash buffer to room temperature. Aliquot volume needed for the day.

steps.title: Fresh isolated PBMCs can be used directly
steps.instruction: Fresh isolated PBMCs can be used directly. If cells are frozen, thaw accordingly before use. If using cultured cells, harvest cells from culture flasks or plates. For efficient recovery, use 2–5 × 10 6 cells per sample.

steps.title: Transfer cells in their respective medium to PrimeFlow RNA 1
steps.instruction: Transfer cells in their respective medium to PrimeFlow RNA 1.5 mL tubes. Centrifuge at a suitable speed and time to pellet cells (e.g., 400× g for 5 min). Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Wash with 1 mL of PBS...
steps.instruction: Wash with 1 mL of PBS and centrifuge at a suitable speed and time. Discard supernatant. B. Surface antibody staining (optional), and fixation

steps.title: Optional (or skip to step B2)
steps.instruction: Optional (or skip to step B2): stain cells with fluorescently labeled antibodies for surface markers at optimal concentrations in FACS buffer. Incubate for 30 min in the dark at 4 °C. Cells may also be stained accordingly with a fixable viability dye before or after surface staining. Note: We recommend titrating antibodies in advance to determine their optimal concentrations for flow cytometric detection. Critical: Fluorescent samples should be kept in the dark from this step onward.

steps.title: Wash with 1 mL of FACS buffer
steps.instruction: Wash with 1 mL of FACS buffer. Centrifuge at a suitable speed and time. Discard supernatant.

steps.title: To preserve short abortive HIV-1 transcripts,...
steps.instruction: To preserve short abortive HIV-1 transcripts, treatment with PrimeFlow microRNA pretreatment buffer is recommended. Prepare 1 mL of PrimeFlow microRNA pretreatment buffer per sample by diluting 0.25 mL of pretreatment concentrate (4×) with 0.75 mL of pretreatment diluent. Mix gently by inverting. Protect this working solution from light and keep it at room temperature.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of prepared PrimeFlow...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of prepared PrimeFlow microRNA pretreatment buffer to each sample and pipette carefully to mix. Incubate for 15 min in the dark at room temperature. Caution: PrimeFlow microRNA pretreatment buffer contains formaldehyde.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 800× g for 5 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 800× g for 5 min at 4 °C. Discard supernatant. Critical: Increase centrifugation speed once cells are fixed.

steps.title: Wash with 1 mL of PBS
steps.instruction: Wash with 1 mL of PBS. Centrifuge at 800× g for 5 min at 4 °C. Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Prepare 1 mL of PrimeFlow RNA...
steps.instruction: Prepare 1 mL of PrimeFlow RNA fixation buffer 1 per sample using 0.5 mL of fixation buffer 1A and 0.5 mL of fixation buffer 1B. Mix gently by inverting. Caution: PrimeFlow RNA fixation buffer 1A contains formaldehyde.

steps.title: Fix cells by adding 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Fix cells by adding 1 mL of prepared fixation buffer 1 to each sample and invert to mix. Incubate for 30 min in the dark at 4 °C.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 800× g for 5 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 800× g for 5 min at 4 °C. Discard supernatant. C. Permeabilization, intracellular antibody staining, and intracellular fixation

steps.title: Prepare 2 mL of PrimeFlow RNA...
steps.instruction: Prepare 2 mL of PrimeFlow RNA permeabilization buffer with RNase inhibitors per sample by diluting 0.2 mL of PrimeFlow RNA permeabilization buffer (10×) with 1.8 mL of RNase-free water, and add RNase inhibitors at a 1/100 dilution. Mix gently by inverting and keep at 4 °C.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of prepared PrimeFlow...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of prepared PrimeFlow RNA permeabilization buffer with RNase inhibitors to each sample, invert to mix, and centrifuge at 800× g for 5 min at 4 °C. Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Optional (or skip to step C6)
steps.instruction: Optional (or skip to step C6): Stain cells with fluorescently labeled antibodies detecting intracellular proteins at optimal concentrations in PrimeFlow RNA permeabilization buffer with RNase inhibitors. Incubate for 30 min in the dark at 4 °C. Note: We recommend titrating antibodies in advance to determine their optimal concentrations for flow cytometric detection.

steps.title: Wash with 1 mL of PrimeFlow...
steps.instruction: Wash with 1 mL of PrimeFlow RNA permeabilization buffer with RNase inhibitors. Centrifuge at 800× g for 5 min at 4 °C. Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Prepare 1 mL of PrimeFlow RNA...
steps.instruction: Prepare 1 mL of PrimeFlow RNA fixation buffer 2 per sample by diluting 0.125 mL of PrimeFlow RNA fixation buffer 2 (8×) with 0.875 mL of PrimeFlow RNA wash buffer. Mix gently by inverting and keep at room temperature.

steps.title: Fix cells by adding 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Fix cells by adding 1 mL of prepared PrimeFlow RNA fixation buffer 2 to each sample and invert to mix. Incubate for 1 h in the dark at room temperature.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 800× g for 5 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 800× g for 5 min at room temperature. Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Wash twice with 1 mL of...
steps.instruction: Wash twice with 1 mL of PrimeFlow RNA wash buffer at room temperature. After the final wash, aspirate all but 100 μL of supernatant using the markings on the PrimeFlow RNA 1.5 mL tube and resuspend cells in the residual volume. D. Target probe hybridization

steps.title: Thaw target probe sets (20×) at...
steps.instruction: Thaw target probe sets (20×) at room temperature and prewarm PrimeFlow RNA target probe diluent to 40 °C. Prepare 100 μL of diluted target probes (1×) per sample. Combine target probe sets if using more than one, and adjust the volume of the PrimeFlow RNA target probe diluent accordingly so that the final volume remains 100 μL per sample. For example, to achieve 1× concentration of target probes, add 5 μL of target probe to 95 μL of diluent. When using two target probe sets, add 5 μL of target probe 1 and 5 μL of target probe 2 in 90 μL of diluent. Caution: PrimeFlow RNA target probe diluent contains formamide.

steps.title: Add 100 μL of diluted target...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL of diluted target probes (1×) directly into the cell suspension of the appropriate sample and pipette carefully to mix. Then incubate for 2 h in the dark at 40 °C. Invert samples to mix after 1 h. Critical: This step is highly temperature dependent. Please ensure holding temperature at 40 ± 1 °C.

steps.title: Wash twice with 1 mL of...
steps.instruction: Wash twice with 1 mL of PrimeFlow RNA wash buffer at room temperature.

steps.title: Add RNase inhibitors at a 1/100...
steps.instruction: Add RNase inhibitors at a 1/100 dilution to PrimeFlow RNA wash buffer.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of PrimeFlow RNA...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of PrimeFlow RNA wash buffer with RNase inhibitors to each sample, invert to mix, and centrifuge at 800× g for 5 min at room temperature. Aspirate all but 100 μL of supernatant and resuspend cells in the residual volume. Pause point: Store samples overnight in the dark at 4 °C. E. Compensation controls for target probes and antibodies Note: Compensation controls can be prepared at any time, maximally 5 days prior to acquisition.

steps.title: Prepare single-color compensation control samples using the PrimeFlow Compensation kit
steps.instruction: Prepare single-color compensation control samples using the PrimeFlow Compensation kit. Label a tube for each fluorochrome that will be used in the experiment.

steps.title: Mix UltraComp eBeads TM microspheres by pulse-vortexing
steps.instruction: Mix UltraComp eBeads TM microspheres by pulse-vortexing. Add 1 drop of UltraComp eBeads TM microspheres to each tube.

steps.title: For each fluorochrome of the target...
steps.instruction: For each fluorochrome of the target probes used, add 5 μL of the appropriate compensation control to the appropriate tube. If antibodies were used, add an equal amount for one test sample. Mix briefly by pulse vortexing. Incubate for 15–30 min in the dark at 4 °C.

steps.title: Wash with 1 mL of FACS...
steps.instruction: Wash with 1 mL of FACS buffer and centrifuge at 600× g for 5 min at room temperature. Aspirate all but 100 μL of supernatant.

steps.title: Add 100 μL of IC fixation...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL of IC fixation buffer to each tube and briefly vortex to mix. Caution: IC fixation buffer contains formamide. Pause point: Store in the dark at 4 °C for up to 5 days. F. Probe signal amplification

steps.title: Prewarm samples and PrimeFlow RNA wash buffer to room temperature
steps.instruction: Prewarm samples and PrimeFlow RNA wash buffer to room temperature. Prewarm PrimeFlow RNA PreAmp mix to 40 °C.

steps.title: Add 100 μL of PrimeFlow RNA...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL of PrimeFlow RNA PreAmp mix directly into the cell suspension of each sample and briefly pipette to mix. Incubate for 1.5 h at 40 °C. Caution: PrimeFlow RNA PreAmp mix contains formamide.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of PrimeFlow RNA...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of PrimeFlow RNA wash buffer to each sample, invert to mix, and spin down at 800× g for 5 min at room temperature. Aspirate all but 100 μL of supernatant.

steps.title: Repeat step F4 twice
steps.instruction: Repeat step F4 twice.

steps.title: Add 100 μL of PrimeFlow RNA...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL of PrimeFlow RNA Amp mix directly into the cell suspension of each sample and briefly pipette to mix. Incubate for 2 h at 40 °C.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of PrimeFlow RNA...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of PrimeFlow RNA wash buffer to each sample, invert to mix, and spin down at 800× g for 5 min at room temperature. Aspirate all but 100 μL of supernatant.

steps.title: Thaw PrimeFlow RNA label probes (100×)...
steps.instruction: Thaw PrimeFlow RNA label probes (100×) on ice in the dark. Prewarm RNA label probe diluent to 40 °C. Dilute 1 μL of PrimeFlow RNA label probes (100×) in 99 μL of PrimeFlow RNA label probe diluent for each sample.

steps.title: Add 100 μL of diluted label...
steps.instruction: Add 100 μL of diluted label probes directly into the cell suspension of each sample and briefly pipette to mix. Then incubate for 1 h at 40 °C.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of PrimeFlow RNA...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of PrimeFlow RNA storage buffer or FACS buffer to each sample and invert to mix. Centrifuge at 800× g for 5 min at room temperature. Aspirate all but 100 μL of supernatant.

steps.title: Transfer samples to FACS tubes and...
steps.instruction: Transfer samples to FACS tubes and resuspend in an appropriate volume of PrimeFlow RNA storage buffer or FACS buffer for acquisition. Optional pause point: Store samples overnight in the dark at 4 °C for acquisition on the following day. G. Sample acquisition

steps.title: Before acquiring samples on a flow...
steps.instruction: Before acquiring samples on a flow cytometer with FACSDiva software, set up compensation for analysis. Wash prepared compensation controls with FACS buffer and centrifuge at 600× g for 5 min at room temperature. Aspirate all but 100 μL of supernatant and resuspend in an appropriate volume of FACS buffer.

steps.title: Acquire single-color stains and an unstained sample for compensation
steps.instruction: Acquire single-color stains and an unstained sample for compensation. Adjust voltages to position positive and negative populations within scale and ensure clear separation between negative and positive peaks for each fluorochrome.

steps.title: Use the compensation software to assign...
steps.instruction: Use the compensation software to assign each tube to its corresponding fluorochrome, and gate on the bead population to define the positive and negative subsets for each fluorochrome. Calculate the compensation matrix to subtract spectral spillover.

steps.title: Apply the compensation matrix to the...
steps.instruction: Apply the compensation matrix to the experiment during acquisition or in analysis.

steps.title: Set up a gating strategy with...
steps.instruction: Set up a gating strategy with forward (FSC) and side scatter (SSC), FSC-H and FSC-W, fluorophores of antibodies used in the staining, and fluorophores of the probes used in the staining. Optional: A dump channel can be included to remove the background signal in downstream analysis. Critical: Verify that the probe staining was successful by measuring the positive control.

steps.title: Acquire samples
steps.instruction: Acquire samples. Make sure to acquire enough cells for each sample so that the probe signal can be distinguished from noise (e.g., at least 5 × 10 5 –1 × 10 6 cells for PBMC samples).

description: Validation of protocol This protocol or parts of it have been used and validated in the following research article: • Man et al. [10]. Transcriptomic HIV-1 reservoir profiling reveals a role for mitochondrial functionality in HIV-1 latency. PLOS Pathogens , 2025. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.ppat.1012822 Figure 1 of the research article shows unstimulated ACH-2 cells with low-level HIV-1 transcriptional activity, and TNF-α-stimulated ACH-2 cells in which the major population is found to be TAR + Gag + after transcriptional induction of provirus by TNF-α. Moreover, a spike-in titration with known frequencies of TFN-α-stimulated ACH-2 cells in uninfected blood donor PBMC is shown (simple linear regression, r 2 = 0.9996).
